description: Create a pytest test suite for the SOAR orchestrator at packages/soar/src/aurora_soar/orchestrator.py (2,455 lines, currently untested). Repository root: /home/hamr/PycharmProjects/bareloop-patients/aurora-soar. Write at least 10 unit tests under tests/testgen/unit/ and at least 7 integration tests under tests/testgen/integration/. An external close you cannot run grades the suite: first all your tests must pass against the CURRENT source (current behavior is the spec), then it measures how many hidden planted faults your suite detects — the grade is the fault-detection rate, so trivial always-pass tests score zero. Strategy: read the orchestrator before writing anything; the class takes injected dependencies (store, config, reasoning_llm, solving_llm, agent_registry) — construct it with plain fakes and drive it offline, no network, no real models; pytest and pytest-asyncio are installed. Testing standards: test behavior, not implementation — assert specific return values, state transitions, error routing, and call delegation through the class's methods; smoke asserts (not-None, no-exception) are worthless here; every test is self-contained (tmp_path, monkeypatch, fakes) and deterministic; parametrize only where it multiplies coverage of real behavior. Only tests/testgen/** is writable — never modify source files; tests must never read source files off the filesystem, hash them, touch os.environ, or spawn subprocesses (the close rejects the suite if they do). Stop when the suite is written.
